
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)

# Set working directory
setwd("C:/Users/ryany/Downloads/picrust2_out/picrust2_out/pathways_out/path_abun_unstrat.tsv")

# Load PICRUSt2 pathway abundance table
path_abun <- read.delim("path_abun_unstrat.tsv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
path_abun <- as.data.frame(t(path_abun))  # Transpose to samples as rows, pathways as columns

# Load metadata
metadata <- read.csv("C:/Users/ryany/Downloads/metadata.csv", row.names = 1)
metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata)

# Print available metadata columns for debugging
cat("Available metadata columns:", colnames(metadata), "\n")

# Ensure sample names match between abundance table and metadata
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(path_abun), rownames(metadata))
path_abun <- path_abun[common_samples, ]
metadata <- metadata[common_samples, ]

# Create phyloseq object
otu_table <- otu_table(path_abun, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
sample_data <- sample_data(metadata)
physeq <- phyloseq(otu_table, sample_data)

# Compute Bray-Curtis distance
dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(physeq, method = "bray")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa <- cmdscale(dist_matrix, k = 2, eig = TRUE)
pcoa_df <- data.frame(SampleID = rownames(pcoa$points), PC1 = pcoa$points[,1], PC2 = pcoa$points[,2])
pcoa_df <- merge(pcoa_df, metadata, by.x = "SampleID", by.y = "row.names")

# Categorize samples by the last digit (e.g., .1, .2, .3) as Plant 1, Plant 2, Plant 3
pcoa_df$Plant_Group <- factor(substr(pcoa_df$SampleID, nchar(pcoa_df$SampleID), nchar(pcoa_df$SampleID)),
                              levels = c("1", "2", "3"),
                              labels = c("Plant 1", "Plant 2", "Plant 3"))

# Visualize PCoA
p <- ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = sample_type, shape = Plant_Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCoA of PICRUSt2 Predicted Pathways", x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Sample Type", shape = "Plant Group") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# Save plot as PNG
ggsave("pcoa_picrust2_pathways.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Optional: Save PCoA results
write.csv(pcoa_df, "pcoa_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
